title: The MIT-IBM Computing Research Lab launches to shape the future of AI and quantum computing
summary: Building on a long-standing MIT–IBM collaboration, the new lab will chart the convergence of AI, algorithms, and quantum computing.
author: MIT Schwarzman College of Computing

title: Enabling privacy-preserving AI training on everyday devices
summary: A new method could bring more accurate and efficient AI models to high-stakes applications like health care and finance, even in under-resourced settings.
author: Adam Zewe | MIT News

title: A faster way to estimate AI power consumption
summary: The “EnergAIzer” method generates reliable results in seconds, enabling data center operators to efficiently allocate resources and reduce wasted energy.
author: Adam Zewe | MIT News

title: MIT scientists build the world’s largest collection of Olympiad-level math problems, and open it to everyone
summary: New dataset of 30,000-plus competition math problems from 47 countries gives AI researchers a harder test — and students worldwide a better training ground.
author: Rachel Gordon | MIT CSAIL

title: Teaching AI models to say “I’m not sure”
summary: A new training method improves the reliability of AI confidence estimates without sacrificing performance, addressing a root cause of hallucination in reasoning models.
author: Rachel Gordon | MIT CSAIL

title: Jacob Andreas and Brett McGuire named Edgerton Award winners
summary: The associate professors of EECS and chemistry, respectively, are honored for exceptional contributions to teaching, research, and service at MIT.
author: Danielle Randall Doughty | Jane Halpern | Department of Chemistry | Department of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science

title: Bringing AI-driven protein-design tools to biologists everywhere
summary: Founded by Tristan Bepler PhD ’20 and former MIT professor Tim Lu PhD ’07, OpenProtein.AI offers researchers open-source models and other tools for protein engineering.
author: Zach Winn | MIT News

title: Helping data centers deliver higher performance with less hardware
summary: Researchers developed a system that intelligently balances workloads to improve the efficiency of flash storage hardware in a data center.
author: Adam Zewe | MIT News

title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure

author: michael.nunez@venturebeat.com (Michael Nuñez)

author: michael.nunez@venturebeat.com (Michael Nuñez)
